title: README claims gorilla/mux but code uses stdlib net/http
evidence: README.md:3 says 'built with Go, SQLite, and gorilla/mux' but go.mod:5 has only mattn/go-sqlite3 and main.go:6 uses net/http routing
suggestion: Correct the README to state stdlib net/http routing

title: PUT /books/{id} response echoes request body, not persisted row
evidence: main.go:253 encodes the decoded `book` whose ID comes from the JSON payload (0 if omitted), not the path `id`; only passes tests because TestUpdateBook sends id=1
suggestion: Set book.ID = id before encoding, or re-query the row after UPDATE

title: Health check does not verify DB connectivity
evidence: main.go:73 healthHandler always returns status 'ok' without pinging db
suggestion: Optionally db.Ping() in the health handler to report real readiness

title: Very low token efficiency for the task size
evidence: scores.json token_efficiency=0.0059 (qwennext80b-cached run); output is a correct single-file API but consumed disproportionate tokens
suggestion: None for code; noted for cross-run cost comparison
